items.title: CMS proposes caps on Medicaid state-directed payments; projects $775B savings over 10 years
items.summary: CMS proposed a rule to cap Medicaid managed-care state-directed provider payment rates at 100% of Medicare rates in expansion states and 110% of Medicare rates in non-expansion states (or 100% of the Medicaid state plan rate when no comparable Medicare rate exists). CMS estimates the proposal would save $775 billion over 10 years ($510 billion federal) and notes SDP spending projected at $107 billion in FY 2024 could rise to $296 billion by FY 2034 without changes.

items.title: Public comments due July 21 on CMS Medicaid payment proposal
items.summary: The Federal Register posting for CMS's proposed rule on Medicaid state-directed payments and targeted fee-for-service practitioner payments lists a public comment deadline of July 21, 2026. Stakeholders who want to weigh in on caps and guardrails for state payment arrangements should submit comments before that date.

items.title: HUD issues new Section 8/HCV guidance to reduce overpayments when tenants die
items.summary: HUD's PIH Notice 2026-11 (issued April 30, 2026) requires Housing Choice Voucher agencies to run the EIV Deceased Tenants Report at least monthly before paying housing assistance payments. The notice sets timeframes such as requesting death confirmation within 24 hours, submitting required HUD-50058 actions within 60 days, and requiring landlords to repay any overpaid assistance within 30 days.
items.source: U.S. Department of Housing and Urban Development

items.title: USDA posts implementation guidance list for SNAP provisions in the One Big Beautiful Bill Act
items.summary: USDA's Food and Nutrition Service updated its One Big Beautiful Bill Act (OBBB) page on May 21, 2026 and lists new SNAP implementation memos tied to FY 2026 changes. The listed materials cover items such as SNAP FY 2026 cost-of-living adjustments (COLA), work-requirement/ABAWD exception guidance, and FY 2026 standard utility allowance (SUA) instructions.

items.title: FAFSA deadline approaching: submit 2025–26 application by June 30
items.summary: For the 2025–26 aid year, the federal deadline to submit a FAFSA is 11:59 p.m. Central Time on June 30, 2026. If you need to make corrections or updates after filing, at least one school aid office guidance notes a corrections deadline of 11:59 p.m. Central Time on Sept. 12, 2026.
items.source: Columbus State Community College (FAFSA guidance referencing Federal Student Aid deadlines)

items.title: IRS outlines 2026 standard deduction amounts under the One Big Beautiful Bill Act
items.summary: IRS guidance on the One Big Beautiful Bill Act says the 2026 standard deduction would be $32,200 for married couples filing jointly, $16,100 for single filers, and $24,150 for heads of household. The page also describes temporary 2025–2028 deductions, including up to a $6,000 additional deduction for taxpayers age 65+ (phasing out above $75,000 MAGI for single filers and $150,000 for joint filers).

items.title: IRS extends Washington disaster tax relief deadlines to Aug. 5, 2026
items.summary: The IRS updated its Washington severe-storms disaster relief announcement to postpone many federal filing and payment deadlines to Aug. 5, 2026 (replacing a previously listed May 1, 2026 date). The relief applies to individual and business returns and payments originally due during the covered period, and the notice references FEMA declaration number 3629-EM.
